_doc: STRICT-CHECK manifest — things the user explicitly asked to see on the site. scrapers/sanity_check.py turns each item into a WARNING_CHECK (name prefixed 'MUST-SURFACE:') and asserts >=`min` matching events in the feed. A miss prints loudly as ⚠ MUST-SURFACE MISSING with its `why` + `hint`, so a requested source silently yielding nothing (stale feed, over-aggressive filter, unmerged fix, dead scraper) is caught instead of quietly dropped. This closes the loop 'what I asked for actually shows up'. Grows as the user asks for specific things — add an item, no code change needed. Matching: case-insensitive substring of any `needle` in any listed `field` (title/description/sourceUrl/account/instagramAccount/source/location; `categories` matches list membership).

items.why: user asked repeatedly why NYC Philosophy Club lu.ma events weren't showing
items.hint: lu.ma/philosophy curator calendar; needs the curated-host shell bypass (commit 8d10fc2) live on main + a scrape that reaches lu.ma

items.why: user: incorporate more fitness-based events + more run clubs (recurring too)
items.hint: Meetup fitness searches + Eventbrite run-club slug + fitness IG seeds; check score floor didn't trim them
dedicated brooklyncontra scraper; DISTINCT_SCHEDULE_SOURCES dedup exemption keeps distinct dates

items.id: MoMA PS1 Warm Up (vetted top organizer)
items.why: user pointed at the Warm Up series as a top-quality vetted Eventbrite organizer
items.hint: Eventbrite organizer /o/8184194121 in ORGANIZER_URLS + user_curated_sources; needs a scrape reaching eventbrite
IG seed; gated on IG sweep working (fb-174) — may only surface via cross-source enrichment until IG unblocks
